issues.title: Correlated subquery
issues.problem: The correlated subquery runs for each row, which is inefficient and can be very slow at scale.
issues.fix: Use a JOIN to avoid the correlated subquery.

issues.title: Alias reference error
issues.problem: The alias 'failed_count' cannot be referenced in the same SELECT clause where it is defined.
issues.fix: Calculate the failure rate in an outer query or a subquery.

issues.title: Integer division
issues.problem: Integer division will truncate the decimal part, leading to incorrect failure rates.
issues.fix: Use decimal division by casting one of the operands to DECIMAL.

issues.title: Potentially missing index
issues.problem: The query could benefit from indexes on transaction_date and status for faster filtering and grouping.
issues.fix: CREATE INDEX idx_fact_transactions_date_status ON fact_transactions(transaction_date, status);

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, which can make it harder to understand for other developers.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose of the query and its logic.

review.corrected_sql:
WITH failed_transactions AS (
  SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) AS failed_count
  FROM fact_transactions
  WHERE status = 'FAILED'
  GROUP BY transaction_date
),
total_transactions AS (
  SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) AS total_count
  FROM fact_transactions
  GROUP BY transaction_date
)
SELECT t.transaction_date,
       f.failed_count,
       t.total_count,
       CAST(f.failed_count AS DECIMAL(10,2)) / t.total_count * 100 AS failure_rate
FROM total_transactions t
LEFT JOIN failed_transactions f ON t.transaction_date = f.transaction_date
ORDER BY t.transaction_date;
review.summary: The query had several correctness issues, including a correlated subquery and integer division. It also lacked comments for readability and could benefit from performance optimizations.
